expertise_area: Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) and AI safety
episode_title: Measuring Progress Towards AGI and the Path to Human-Level AI

key_insights.insight: Measuring AGI progress requires a broad suite of tests across many cognitive tasks, not just single benchmarks.
key_insights.quote: So if we want to test whether we're meeting this threshold or we're getting close to the threshold, what we actually need then is a lot of different kinds of measurements and tests that spans the breadth of all the sorts of cognitive tasks that people can do, and then to have a sense of what is human performance on these sorts of tasks, and that then allows us to sort of judge whether or not we're there.

key_insights.insight: Current benchmarks like MMLU miss important human abilities such as understanding streaming video and episodic memory.
key_insights.quote: They don't measure things like understanding streaming video, for example, because these are language models and people can do things like understanding streaming video. They don't do things like humans have what we call episodic memory.

key_insights.insight: Addressing limitations like episodic memory requires architectural changes, not just scaling up models.
key_insights.quote: I think it'll be architectural in nature because the, well, the current architectures, they don't really have what you need to do this.

key_insights.insight: True creativity in AI requires search through possibility spaces, not just blending of training data.
key_insights.quote: To do something is truly creative, there's not just a blending of existing things. That requires searching through a space of possibilities and finding these hidden gems that are sort of the hidden away in there somewhere.

key_insights.insight: Aligning advanced AI demands system‑2‑style reasoning with a deep world model and ethical understanding.
key_insights.quote: You need a system which has a deep understanding of the world, has a good world model, it is a good understanding of people, it has a good understanding of ethics, and it has robust and very reliable reasoning.

key_insights.insight: The next major milestone in AI will be fully multimodal models that integrate text, image, video, and action.
key_insights.quote: I think the next landmark that people will think back to and remember is going much more fully multimodal, I think. Because I think that will open out the sort of understanding that you see in language models into a much larger space of possibilities.

actionable_advice.advice: Develop a comprehensive suite of cognitive benchmarks covering diverse human abilities such as video understanding and episodic memory.
actionable_advice.how_to: Create standardized tests that assess performance across multiple domains and compare to human baselines.

actionable_advice.advice: Integrate hippocampal‑style memory modules into neural architectures to enable rapid learning of specific information.
actionable_advice.how_to: Add a fast‑weight memory component alongside the slow‑learned weights, allowing the model to store and recall recent experiences.

actionable_advice.advice: Combine foundation models with explicit search algorithms to achieve genuine creativity beyond data mimicry.
actionable_advice.how_to: Use the model as a world model and run Monte‑Carlo tree search or similar planning to explore novel action sequences.

actionable_advice.advice: Implement system‑2‑style reasoning with explicit ethical analysis before acting.
actionable_advice.how_to: Prompt the AI to generate step‑by‑step reasoning, evaluate outcomes using a verified world model, and apply an ethical framework to choose actions.

contrarian_claims.claim: Architectural changes, not just scaling, are required to solve episodic memory limitations in LLMs.
contrarian_claims.context: Many researchers believe that simply increasing model size and training data will overcome memory shortcomings, but Legg argues the current architecture lacks the necessary mechanisms.

contrarian_claims.claim: Domain‑specific projects like AlphaFold are orthogonal to the main path toward AGI.
contrarian_claims.context: Some view such breakthroughs as stepping stones to general intelligence, yet Legg sees them as separate endeavors that do not directly contribute to AGI development.

contrarian_claims.claim: There are no fundamental blockers to achieving AGI; progress will come from continued research and engineering.
contrarian_claims.context: Contrary to claims that deep learning hits inherent limits, Legg sees clear paths forward for addressing shortcomings like delusions, factuality, and memory.
